subject: Application for System Engineer - Optical Ground Station
body_paragraphs: I am writing to express my enthusiasm for the system Engineer position at QTlabs. As a Photonics Engineer currently concluding my PhD in Innsbruck, I want to pivot my technical expertise with adaptive optics, optics setups, and computational pipelines. I can contribute to your mission of securing global networks through robust Optical Ground Stations (OGS).

Although my recent research focuses on correcting optical aberrations, quantum physics and laser knowledge remain at the core of my background. My experience and knowledge can be transferred to the challenges of your OGS team.

\textbf{Adaptive Optics \& Turbulence Mitigation:} \\ Your role requires expertise dealing with atmospheric turbulence. During my PhD, I implemented adaptive optics using hardware/software to compensate aberrations induced by tissue scattering. This skill is transferable to atmospheric distortions in satellite-to-ground links.

\textbf{Advanced Detection \& Noise Management:} \\ I understand that high-performance OGS solutions rely on ultra-low-noise detection. My work with advanced optical microscopy systems (two-photon absorption and Raman) involved detecting few counts from weak signals similar to single-photon detection.

\textbf{System Integration \& Computer Vision:} \\ Beyond the lab, I explored the industry in Medellin where I worked with machine/computer vision systems, dealing with industrial/NIR/VIS cameras, illumination techniques and intelligent computational systems. These experiences provides me with the algorithmic foundation necessary for the tracking and pointing control concepts for Low-Earth Orbit satellites.

About me, I am Colombian living in Innsbruck with a German Master's in Photonics, and upcoming PhD in optical microscopy and adaptive optics. After finishing my PhD, I will get an Austrian graduated working permit that makes the hiring process seamless to you, equivalent to EU citizen. I am available after april/may and I am open to chat how my expertise in optics and solving problems can support the QTlabs mission. I hope you enjoyed the description and thank you for your time.

resume.summary: Optical Systems Engineer with specialization in Adaptive Optics (AO). Expertise in correcting optical aberrations in microscopy, transferable to Free-Space Optical communications. My background spans nonlinear optics (two-photon microscopy), hands-on GPU-accelerated simulations and machine vision tracking.

competencies.details: Wavefront control: Design of closed-loop AO systems, involving microscopic optical setups such as spatial light modulators, lasers and NIR optics. Knowledge in optical modeling and simulations in scattering media such as atmosphere. Also aware of ISO implementations.
Nonlinear optics: Managing signal-to-noise ratios in photon-starved regimes (analogous to QKD single-photon). Hardware: Photomultipliers (PMTs), spectrometers, single fibers.
Computing: High-performance image processing pipelines using Python(Jax) for GPU acceleration. Embedded: FPGA (LabView) hands-on for real-time control.

bullets: \textbf{Adaptive Optics} Engineered control systems for optical microscopes to correct severe aberrations induced by deep biological tissue.
\textbf{Nonlinear system design:} Two-Photon microscopes implementation. Balanced high-power lasers (analogous to Beacon lasers) with efficient and sensitive detection.
\textbf{Lab Management:} Maintained high-precision optical infrastructure, ensuring operational standards similar to OGS facility requirements.
\textbf{System Prototyping:} Assembled vibrational imaging prototypes with the integration of spectrometers, fiber probes and CW lasers.
\textbf{Data Analysis:} Data analysis using machine learning methods to identify signal patterns from noise datasets.
\textbf{Tracking Systems:} Developed industrial quality control systems using computer vision, industrial cameras, and illumination control.
\textbf{Safety-Critical Logic:} Managed product lifecycles for safety-critical inspection systems.

publications_awards: Muñoz-Bolaños, J. D., et al. Design of a dispersive 1064 nm fiber probe Raman imaging spectrometer and its application to human bladder resectates. Applied Sciences, 14(11), 4726 (2024).
Muñoz-Bolaños, J. D. et al. Confocal Raman Microscopy with Adaptive Optics. ACS Photonics 12(1), 176–184 (2025).
Sohmen, M., Muñoz-Bolaños, J. D., et al. Optofluidic adaptive optics in multi-photon microscopy. Biomedical Optics Express 14(4), 1562–1578 (2023).

references.name: Prof. Monika Ritsch-Marte
references.role: Head of Institute for Biomedical Physics
